lessons.title: Sports
lessons.topic: Choosing a sport to play at the weekend
lessons.context: Choosing a sport to play at the weekend.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Where do you play sports?
questions.hint: Think about where you have lots of space to run and play!

questions.question_text: What do you wear when you play sports?
questions.hint: You need clothes that help you move easily and run fast!

questions.question_text: When do many children play sports?
questions.hint: Think about when you don't have school and have free time to play!

items.definition: A game with a big orange ball.
A game with a ball and goals.
A game with rackets and a ball.
A fun activity to play.
A sport where you bounce an orange ball and throw it into a high net
A sport where you kick a round ball with your feet
A sport where you hit a small ball with a racket over a net
Something fun you play with rules, like a sport or video game

steps.title: Tom's Weekend Sport
steps.text: Tom always plays sports on Saturday. He loves football and basketball. His sister Emma plays tennis every weekend. Today, Tom wants to play a new game with his friends in the park.
steps.scene_description: happy children playing different sports together in a sunny park on weekend

questions.question: When does Tom always play sports?
What sport does Emma play?
Where does Tom want to play today?

steps.explanation: We use 'play' with ball sports, 'go' when we move to do sports, and 'do' with other sports. We use 'always' for things we do every time and 'never' for things we don't do.
examples: I always go swimming on Sunday.

items.question: I ___ football every Saturday.
items.explanation: We use 'play' with ball sports like football

items.question: She ___ swimming on Sunday.
items.explanation: We use 'go' with -ing sports, and 'goes' with she

items.question: They ___ karate at school.
items.explanation: We use 'do' with karate and other martial arts

items.question: I ___ play tennis.
items.explanation: 'Never' means you don't do something at any time

items.question: He ___ plays basketball.
items.explanation: 'Always' means you do something every time

steps.audio_text: Hello children! My name is Sam. I always play sports on the weekend. On Saturday, I play football with my friends in the park. On Sunday, I play basketball at school. I never play tennis because I don't have a racket. What's your favorite game?

questions.question: What sport does Sam play on Saturday?
Where does Sam play basketball?
Why does Sam never play tennis?

steps.prompt: Now it's your turn to speak! Tell me about the sports you play.

prompts.prompt: Say: I play ___ on the weekend.
prompts.expected_text: I play football on the weekend.

prompts.prompt: Say: I always play ___ or I never play ___.
Tell me: What sport do you want to play next weekend?
prompts.expected_text: I want to play tennis next weekend.

questions.question_text: Which emoji shows 'basketball'?
I ___ football every Saturday.
She ___ swimming on Sunday.
True or False: 'I always play tennis' means I play tennis every time.
Tom plays football on Saturday. When does Tom play football?

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you like to do after school?
questions.hint: Think about fun things you do when school finishes!

questions.question_text: Where do children go to do hobbies together?
questions.hint: It's a special place where friends meet to do fun activities!

questions.question_text: Which activity can you do at an arts club?
questions.hint: Think about what you use crayons and brushes for!

items.definition: A musical instrument with strings.
A big instrument with keys you press.
To use color to make pictures.
items.example: I want to paint a tree.

items.definition: A picture taken with a camera.
items.example: This is a photo of me.

items.hint: You play music with this. It has strings.
You press keys to make music with this.
You use brushes and colors to make art.
You take this with a camera to remember things.

items.sentence: I play the ___ at music club.
Sarah plays the ___ with both hands.
We ___ colorful pictures at art club.
Let's take a ___ of our class!

steps.title: Our Hobby Club
steps.text: Emma goes to hobby club after school. She always plays the piano on Monday. Tom plays the guitar. Lily likes to paint pictures. They never do homework at the club! On Friday, they take a photo together.
steps.scene_description: diverse children doing music and art activities in a bright classroom after school

questions.question: What does Emma always do on Monday?
Do the children do homework at hobby club?
When do the children take a photo?

steps.explanation: We use 'always' when we do something every time, and 'never' when we don't do something at all.
examples: I always play the piano on Monday.
We always go to hobby club after school.

items.correctSentence: She never goes to hobby club
We always do art on Monday

items.sentence: I ___ play the guitar at club.
She ___ goes to music club on Friday.
They ___ to hobby club after school.
Me never go to art club.
I play two guitar at home.

items.question: Emma ___ plays the guitar on Monday.
items.explanation: Use 'always' to show it happens every time

items.question: I ___ go to hobby club. I don't like it.
items.explanation: Use 'never' when you don't do something at all

items.question: We ___ the piano at music club.
She ___ art after school.
items.explanation: Use 'does' with activities like art, homework

items.question: They always ___ to hobby club on Friday.
items.explanation: Use 'go' when moving to a place

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Today I want to tell you about our hobby club. We have hobby club every day after school. On Monday and Wednesday, you can play the piano or the guitar in the music room. On Tuesday and Thursday, we always paint pictures in the art room. On Friday, we take a photo of all the children. I never miss Friday! It's my favorite day.

questions.question: What do children do on Monday?
When do children paint pictures?
What happens on Friday?

steps.prompt: Practice talking about hobby club! Say these sentences out loud.
steps.expected_text: I always go to hobby club.

prompts.prompt: Say: I always go to hobby club.
prompts.expected_text: I always go to hobby club.

prompts.prompt: Say: I play the guitar at music club.
prompts.expected_text: I play the guitar at music club.

prompts.prompt: Say: We never paint on Monday.

questions.question_text: What do you use to make music with strings?
I ___ play the piano after school.
She ___ to hobby club on Friday.
True or False: We use 'play' with musical instruments like guitar and piano.
What do children do at art club?

steps.instruction: Let's talk about things we do every day! Answer these questions about your hobbies!

questions.question_text: What do you like to play every day?
questions.hint: Think about what makes you happy! What do you play with at home?

questions.question_text: When do you usually play outside?
questions.hint: When is it sunny and you have free time to run and play?

questions.question_text: How often do you read a story book?
questions.hint: Think about bedtime! Does someone read to you before you sleep?

items.definition: Every time, without fail.
Not at any time.
Not always; only at times.
items.example: We sometimes go to the park.

items.definition: A machine that plays sound.

steps.title: Emma's Happy Day
steps.text: Emma loves music! She always listens to the radio in the morning. She sometimes plays with her toys after school. Emma likes to go to the park on sunny days. She never goes to bed late because she feels tired.
steps.scene_description: happy young girl listening to radio in bright bedroom, smiling child with toy radio

questions.question: When does Emma listen to the radio?
Does Emma always play with her toys after school?
Why does Emma never go to bed late?

steps.explanation: We use special words like 'always', 'sometimes', and 'never' to tell how often we do our hobbies!
examples: I always play with my toys after school.
I sometimes go to the park on Saturday.
I never do homework on Sunday.

items.correctSentence: He always listens to the radio

items.sentence: She ___ to the park every day.

items.explanation: Use 'goes' with she, he, or it
Use 'play' with I, you, we, or they

items.sentence: He can goes to the park.
We have two radio at home.
items.explanation: Use 'radios' for more than one radio

items.sentence: I always play with my friends.
items.corrected: I always play with my friends.

items.explanation: Use 'goes' with she, he, or it

items.sentence: He always listen to the radio.
items.corrected: He always listens to the radio.
items.explanation: Use 'listens' with he, she, or it

items.question: I ___ play outside every day.
items.explanation: 'Always' shows how often you do something

items.question: She never ___ to bed late.
items.explanation: Use 'goes' with she, he, or it

items.question: We sometimes ___ homework together.
items.explanation: Use 'do' with I, you, we, or they

items.question: He ___ listens to the radio.
items.explanation: 'Sometimes' means not always, but a few times

items.question: They always ___ soccer after school.
items.explanation: Use 'play' with I, you, we, or they

steps.audio_text: Hello everyone! Let me tell you about my friend Tom. Tom always plays with his toy cars after school. He sometimes listens to the radio with his dad in the morning. But Tom never goes swimming because he doesn't like water. What about you? What do you always do?

questions.question: What does Tom always do after school?
When does Tom listen to the radio?
Does Tom go swimming?

steps.prompt: Let's talk about what you do! Practice saying these sentences about your hobbies.
steps.expected_text: I always play with my toys.

prompts.prompt: Tell me something you always do after school. Say: I always...
prompts.expected_text: I always play with my toys.

prompts.prompt: Tell me something you sometimes do on Saturday. Say: I sometimes...
prompts.expected_text: I sometimes go to the park.

prompts.prompt: Tell me something you never do. Say: I never...
prompts.expected_text: I never go to bed late.

questions.question_text: Which word means 'every time, all the time'?
She ___ goes to the park on Sunday.
I sometimes ___ homework after school.
True or False: 'He never play soccer' is correct.
Tom always listens to the radio. Does Tom listen to the radio every day?

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: How many days are in one week?
What is a hobby?
questions.correct_answer: Something fun you like to do
options: Something fun you like to do
questions.hint: It's an activity you enjoy doing in your free time!

questions.question_text: Which day comes after Saturday?
questions.hint: It's the last day of the week!

items.definition: A sport you play with a ball and hoop
A sport you play with your feet and a ball
A musical instrument with strings
A musical instrument with black and white keys
To make pictures with colors and a brush
Every time, all the time
Not ever, zero times
Not always, but not never
A sport with a racket and yellow ball
A fun activity you play
A picture you take with a camera
A device that plays music and talk
A sport you play with a ball and hoop
A musical instrument with strings
Every time, all the time
Not always, but not never

items.question: What do you do with a brush and colors?
Which one has black and white keys?
Which sport do you play with your feet?
Which one plays music and talk?

steps.title: Emma's Week
steps.text: Emma loves hobbies! She always plays basketball on Monday. On Wednesday, she never goes swimming, but she does play the piano. Emma sometimes paints on Friday. On Sunday, she always plays football with her dad.
steps.scene_description: young girl with weekly schedule calendar showing different hobby activities

questions.question: What does Emma do on Monday?
Does Emma go swimming on Wednesday?
Who does Emma play football with on Sunday?

steps.rule_pattern: I ___ play/go/do + hobby. I always/never ___ on ___day.
steps.explanation: We use 'play' for sports with a ball, 'go' with '-ing' words, and 'do' for other activities. We use 'always' when we do something every time and 'never' when we don't do it at all.
examples: I play basketball on Monday. I always play after school.
I go swimming on Tuesday. I never go on Sunday.
I do homework on Wednesday. I always do it before dinner.

steps.audio_text: Hello, children! Let me tell you about my week. I always play basketball on Monday after school. On Wednesday, I never play tennis because I go swimming instead. I sometimes paint pictures on Friday. On Saturday, I always do my favorite hobby - I play the guitar!

questions.question: What does the teacher always do on Monday?
What does the teacher do on Wednesday?
When does the teacher sometimes paint pictures?

steps.prompt: Tell me about your hobbies! Say these sentences out loud.
steps.expected_text: I always play basketball on Monday.

prompts.prompt: Say: I always play basketball on Monday.
prompts.expected_text: I always play basketball on Monday.

prompts.prompt: Say: I sometimes go swimming on Wednesday.
prompts.expected_text: I sometimes go swimming on Wednesday.

prompts.prompt: Now tell me: What hobby do you always do? Say: I always...

questions.question_text: I ___ basketball every Monday.
She ___ swimming on Tuesday.
Which word means 'every time, all the time'?
True or False: We use 'play' with the piano.
What is the correct sentence?
